title: Spec reconciliation: RPL/network documents (appendix-rpl B.1, 04-network, SRH ordering, hop-limit)
description: (1) spec/appendix-rpl.md:18 rank formula says Rank = (PathETX * 128) + MinHopRankIncrease; all three implementations use etx * MinHopRankIncrease (= etx*256). Impls mutually consistent; spec is the outlier - which is canonical? (2) SRH address ordering: Python's insert/advance stores addresses in FORWARD order with next hop at addresses[len-segments_left] and performs no RFC 8200 swap (routing.py:118-180); RFC 6554/8754 use the reversed list. Python-only today - when Rust/C add insert/advance, which convention is LICHEN's profile? Needs a normative answer + SRH test vector before a second implementation lands. (3) Hop-limit decrement on unicast forwarding: RFC 8200 requires it; spec 04-network.md:178-258 mandates it only for broadcast relay; all three stacks currently omit it for unicast. Intent? (4) Trickle canonical values: see mmgu.8 - the spec values (4096/8/10) match no runtime and the advertised dio_int_min=3 matches nothing; pick one regime.
